steps.title: Identify why early snow increases net CO2
steps.content: The researchers conclude that earlier snow cover increases net CO2. The decisive clue is understanding the timing: what creates the condition where early snow leads to more CO2 output?

steps.title: Match the mechanism
steps.content:
Choice D explains it: snow insulation allows heterotrophic respiration to continue while plants are dormant. This accounts for the net CO2 increase—soil microbes release CO2 during a period when plants cannot absorb it. The other choices either focus on different factors (solar radiation effects in A), reverse the effect (B: early snow lowers respiration), or address the wrong aspect (C: snow persistence rather than arrival timing).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
